expressions.english: cook, chef
recipe book, cookbook
ingredients of the dish
cooking school
cooking skill
cooking utensil
Are you good at cooking?
expressions.notes: 잘하다 = to be good at something

expressions.english: I'm not very good at cooking.
expressions.notes:
잘 = well; carefully
요리를 하다 = to cook
못하다 = to be bad at something; to be poor at something

expressions.english: Are there any dishes you can cook?
expressions.notes: -(으)로 줄 알다 = to know how to + V

expressions.english: I'm really interested in cooking.
expressions.notes:
관심 = interest
관심이 많다 = to be interested in + N + very much

expressions.english: I've tried following the cookbook.
expressions.notes:
요리 책 = recipe book, cookbook
따라하다 = to copy (someone); to imitate; to mimic
-아/어/여 보다 = to try + V-ing

expressions.english: I want to be a famous chef.
expressions.notes:
유명하다 = to be famous
유명한 is the adjective form of 유명하다.
요리사 = chef, cook
되다 = to become
-고 싶다 = to want to + V

expressions.english: These days, I've suddenly taken an interest in cooking.
expressions.notes:
요즘 = these days, lately
부쩍 = (something changed) markedly; suddenly
재미 = fun
재미를 붙이다 = to become interested in, take an interest in

expressions.english: My mother is very good at cooking.
expressions.notes:
저희 = (polite) we; us; our
어머니 = mother
참 = very, quite

expressions.english: I finally got a certificate for Korean cuisine.
expressions.notes:
드디어 = finally
한식 = Korean food, Korean cuisine
자격증 = certificate
자격증을 따다 = to get a certificate

expressions.korean: 요리를 할 때는 항상 불을 조심해야 돼요.
expressions.english: When you cook, you always have to be careful with fire.
expressions.notes:
항상 = always, all the time
불 = fire
조심하다 = to be careful
조심하다 = to be careful

expressions.korean: 오랜만에 제 요리 실력을 발휘해 봤어요.
expressions.english: It's been a while since I've showed off my cooking skills.
expressions.notes:
오랜만에 = first time in a long period of time
발휘하다 = to demonstrate/show one's skills

expressions.english: There are more cooking shows now than before.
expressions.notes:
같자기 = all of a sudden, suddenly
프로그램 = program, TV show
-아이/어이/아이지다 = to become + adjective

expressions.english: A famous chef has opened a new restaurant.
expressions.notes:
새 = new
레스토랑 = restaurant
A Korean word for a "restaurant" is "식당," but when you use "레스토랑" instead of "식당," it usually refers to a fancy or upscale restaurant that sells western-style cuisine, including Italian or French. Even if a restaurant is very fancy and expensive, if it's a Korean, Japanese, or Chinese restaurant, normally the word "레스토랑" is not used.
오픈하다 = to open a shop; there's a new shop opened.

expressions.korean: 해물 요리는 집에서 만들기가 조금 번거로워요.
expressions.english: It's really a hassle to make seafood dishes at home.
expressions.notes:
해물 = seafood
요리를 만들다 = to cook
조금 = a little
번거롭다 = to be hassling; to be inconvenient
